summary: Deployed the repo-managed Headscale control plane on the Proxmox host, published headscale.lv3.org through the shared NGINX edge, migrated the Proxmox host and one operator workstation to the new mesh, and verified the approved 10.10.10.0/24 subnet route over the Headscale-managed network.

verification.observed: `headscale nodes list -o json` on `proxmox_florin` showed the `ops@` nodes `proxmox-florin-subnet-router` (`100.64.0.1`) and `florins-macbook-pro` (`100.64.0.3`).

verification.check: Host SSH and Proxmox API over the new mesh IP
verification.observed: `ssh ops@100.64.0.1 hostname` succeeded and `curl -skI https://100.64.0.1:8006/` returned `HTTP/1.1 501 method 'HEAD' not available`, confirming the host-side API listener was reachable over the Headscale path.

verification.check: Approved subnet route to the private guest network
verification.observed: The operator workstation netmap showed `10.10.10.0/24` as the `PrimaryRoutes` entry for `proxmox-florin-subnet-router`, and `curl -skI https://10.10.10.10/ -H 'Host: nginx.lv3.org'` returned `HTTP/2 200` over the routed guest path.

notes: The first live cutover exposed that Headscale 0.28.0 did not auto-promote the advertised subnet route into `approved_routes`, so the `proxmox_headscale` role now reconciles route approval when the managed node already exists.
A temporary public management allow-list entry for `90.95.39.175` was used during recovery after a rescue boot and was removed again before finalizing the workstream.
